inputs.help: Your current age in years

inputs.label: Weight
inputs.help: Your current body weight in kilograms

inputs.label: Height
inputs.help: Your height in centimetres

faq.q: What are macros?
faq.a: Macros (macronutrients) are the three main nutrients your body needs in large quantities: protein, carbohydrates, and fat. Each provides energy (calories) and plays different roles in your body. Protein builds and repairs muscle, carbs fuel activity, and fat supports hormones and cell health.

faq.q: How much protein do I need per day?
faq.a: For most active Australians, 1.6-2.2g of protein per kilogram of body weight is recommended. This calculator uses 2g/kg as a solid baseline for anyone exercising regularly. If you're sedentary, 1.2-1.6g/kg may be sufficient.

faq.q: What is TDEE?
faq.a: TDEE (Total Daily Energy Expenditure) is the total number of calories your body burns in a day, including your basal metabolic rate plus calories burned through activity. It's the starting point for calculating how much you should eat to lose, maintain, or gain weight.

faq.q: Should I track macros or just calories?
faq.a: Tracking macros gives you more control over body composition. Two people eating the same calories can get very different results depending on their macro split. If your goal is to build muscle or lose fat while preserving muscle, tracking macros is significantly more effective than calories alone.

faq.q: How accurate is this macro calculator?
faq.a: This calculator uses the Mifflin-St Jeor equation, which research shows is the most accurate BMR formula for most people. However, individual metabolism varies. Use these numbers as a starting point and adjust based on your actual results over 2-3 weeks.

title: Daily Serves Calculator (Five Food Groups)
description: Find out how many serves of vegetables, fruit, grains, protein, and dairy you need daily based on Australian Dietary Guidelines.
metaDescription: Free Australian daily serves calculator based on the Eat for Health guidelines. Find recommended serves of vegetables, fruit, grains, protein, and dairy for your age and gender.

faq.q: What are the five food groups in Australia?
faq.a: The Australian Dietary Guidelines identify five core food groups: vegetables and legumes, fruit, grain (cereal) foods, lean meats and protein alternatives, and milk/yoghurt/cheese (dairy). Each group provides different essential nutrients, and eating the recommended serves from all five groups supports overall health.

faq.q: What counts as one serve of vegetables?
faq.a: One standard serve of vegetables is about 75g, which equals roughly ½ cup of cooked vegetables, 1 cup of raw salad, or ½ medium potato. Most Australians don't eat enough vegetables — the average adult needs 5-6 serves per day.

faq.q: Why do pregnant women need more grains?
faq.a: Pregnant women need 8.5 serves of grain foods per day (compared to 6 for non-pregnant women) because grains provide essential B vitamins, iron, and energy needed to support the growing baby. Wholegrain and high-fibre options are recommended for at least two-thirds of grain serves.

faq.q: Do these guidelines apply to children under 2?
faq.a: No. The Australian Dietary Guidelines provide specific food group serves for ages 2 and above. Children under 2 have different nutritional needs, including continued breastfeeding or formula. Speak with your paediatrician or child health nurse for age-appropriate feeding advice.

faq.q: Are these guidelines from the Australian Government?
faq.a: Yes. This calculator is based on the Australian Dietary Guidelines published by the National Health and Medical Research Council (NHMRC) through the Eat for Health program. These are the official dietary recommendations for Australians.

faq.q: How much does a home-cooked meal cost in Australia?
faq.a: The average home-cooked dinner in Australia costs $10-$15 for a family of four using standard supermarket ingredients. This can drop to $6-$8 per meal with budget planning, or rise to $20+ with premium ingredients. The key variable is whether you shop at Aldi, Coles, or Woolworths, and whether you meal plan.

faq.q: Are meal kits cheaper than cooking from scratch?
faq.a: Generally no. Meal kits like HelloFresh cost around $8-$12 per person per serve in Australia, which is more expensive than cooking from scratch for most families. However, meal kits reduce food waste and save planning time. For singles or couples, the cost difference is smaller and meal kits can actually save money compared to buying ingredients that go to waste.

faq.q: How much does the average Australian family spend on takeaway?
faq.a: Australian families spend an average of $60-$100 per week on takeaway and food delivery. With delivery app fees and surcharges, the average Uber Eats order for a family of four is around $70-$90. Cutting just one takeaway meal per week can save $3,000-$5,000 per year.

faq.q: What is the cheapest way to feed a family in Australia?
faq.a: Batch cooking and meal planning with supermarket specials is the most cost-effective approach. Shop at Aldi for staples, buy seasonal produce, cook in bulk on weekends, and use a freezer. A family of four can eat well for $120-$150 per week with disciplined planning.

faq.q: Does this calculator include lunch and breakfast?
faq.a: This calculator focuses on dinner meals as that's where the biggest cost variation occurs between home cooking, meal kits, and takeaway. You can adjust the "meals per week" number to include other meals if you want a more complete picture.

title: Weekly Grocery Budget Planner
description: Plan your weekly grocery budget based on household size, dietary needs, and location in Australia.
metaDescription: Plan your Australian grocery budget. Get a realistic weekly spending target based on your household size, dietary needs, and location. Updated for 2025-26 prices.
keywords: how much to spend on groceries australia

faq.q: How much should a family of 4 spend on groceries in Australia?
faq.a: A family of four in metro Australia typically spends $250-$400 per week on groceries in 2025-26, depending on budget style. Budget-conscious families can manage around $220/week by shopping at Aldi, using specials, and meal planning. Comfortable spending sits around $360-$400/week with premium brands and organic produce.

faq.q: Why are groceries more expensive in regional and remote areas?
faq.a: Transport costs, limited competition, and supply chain logistics push grocery prices higher outside capital cities. Regional areas typically pay 5-10% more, while remote communities can pay 20-30% more for basic groceries. This is recognised by the Australian Government through remote area allowances.

faq.q: Is a vegetarian diet cheaper than eating meat?
faq.a: Generally yes. Plant-based proteins like lentils, chickpeas, and tofu are significantly cheaper per kilogram than meat in Australian supermarkets. A vegetarian diet typically costs 10-15% less than a standard diet. Vegan diets can save even more, though specialty vegan products (like plant-based cheese) can offset some savings.

faq.q: Why does a gluten-free diet cost more?
faq.a: Gluten-free specialty products (bread, pasta, flour, snacks) typically cost 2-3 times more than their standard equivalents in Australian supermarkets. While naturally gluten-free foods (rice, potatoes, meat, vegetables) are the same price, the premium on processed gluten-free products adds roughly 15-20% to a typical grocery bill.

faq.q: How can I reduce my grocery spending?
faq.a: The biggest savings come from meal planning, shopping at Aldi for staples, buying seasonal produce, cooking from scratch, and reducing food waste. Using apps like Frugl to compare prices and shopping supermarket specials can save 20-30%. Batch cooking on weekends also reduces the temptation to order takeaway.

title: What Are The Odds Calculator
description: Calculate the probability of random events, from winning a bet to getting struck by lightning.
metaDescription: What are the odds calculator — find the probability of everyday and ridiculous events happening. Fun probability calculator for bets, trivia, and curiosity.

options.label: 🎂 2 people in room of 23 share birthday

inputs.label: Custom: 1 in X chance
inputs.help: Enter the denominator — e.g. 100 for a 1-in-100 chance

inputs.label: Number of Attempts
inputs.help: How many times will you try?

faq.q: How does this calculator work?
faq.a: For a single attempt, the probability is simply 1/X. For multiple attempts, it calculates 1 minus the probability of failing every time: 1 - ((X-1)/X)^n, where n is the number of attempts.

faq.q: What are the odds of winning Oz Lotto?
faq.a: About 1 in 45 million for the jackpot (Division 1). You are roughly 10 times more likely to be struck by lightning in your lifetime than to win the jackpot from a single ticket.

faq.q: Does trying more times guarantee success?
faq.a: No. More attempts improve your cumulative probability, but each individual attempt has the same odds. Even with 1000 lottery tickets, your overall chance is still tiny.

howItWorks: Pick a wild scenario — winning Oz Lotto, getting struck by lightning, finding a four-leaf clover — or enter your own odds. We'll tell you the real probability and how many attempts you'd need before luck is on your side. Great for settling pub debates.

faq.q: How many times do you blink per day?
faq.a: The average person blinks about 15-20 times per minute while awake, which works out to around 15,000-20,000 blinks per day (assuming 16 waking hours).

faq.q: How many breaths does a person take in a lifetime?
faq.a: At an average of 12-20 breaths per minute, a person who lives to 80 takes roughly 672 million to 1.12 billion breaths in their lifetime.

faq.q: Are these numbers accurate?
faq.a: These are estimates based on medical averages. Individual numbers vary based on fitness, activity level, health, and lifestyle. They are fun approximations, not precise measurements.

howItWorks: Your body has been quietly racking up some truly absurd numbers since the day you were born. Heartbeats, blinks, breaths, meals, hours slept — this calculator tallies up your lifetime stats. The results are either fascinating or existentially terrifying. Possibly both.

faq.q: How many days until Christmas?
faq.a: This calculator counts down to December 25 of the current year. If Christmas has already passed this year, it counts down to next year's Christmas.

faq.q: Does this account for leap years?
faq.a: Yes. The calculator uses actual calendar dates, so leap years are handled automatically.

faq.q: Can I count down to any date?
faq.a: Yes. Select "Custom date" and enter any future date. You can count down to weddings, holidays, retirement, exams, or anything else.

howItWorks: Whether you're counting down to Christmas, dreading the end of financial year, or just need to know exactly how many sleeps until your next holiday — this is your calculator. Pick a date or use one of our quick picks. Time waits for no one, but at least now you know how much of it you've got.

title: Death by Caffeine Calculator
description: How many coffees, energy drinks, or cans of cola would it take? Dark humour meets science.
metaDescription: Death by caffeine calculator — how many coffees, Red Bulls, or Cokes would it theoretically take to reach a lethal dose? Fun (and morbid) caffeine calculator.
keywords: how much coffee is too much

faq.q: What is the lethal dose of caffeine?
faq.a: The commonly cited lethal dose (LD50) is approximately 150-200mg per kilogram of body weight. For a 75kg person, that is roughly 11,250-15,000mg. However, serious side effects can occur at much lower doses (around 400-500mg for some people).

faq.q: Can you actually die from caffeine?
faq.a: Caffeine overdose deaths are extremely rare but have occurred, usually from caffeine pills or powder, not beverages. Your stomach would likely reject the volume of liquid long before you reached a dangerous dose from coffee or energy drinks.

faq.q: How much caffeine is safe per day?
faq.a: Health authorities generally recommend no more than 400mg per day for healthy adults — roughly 3-4 cups of coffee. Pregnant women are advised to stay under 200mg. Children and teenagers should consume significantly less.

howItWorks: We've all said "I could literally die for a coffee right now." But how many coffees would it actually take? This calculator works it out based on your body weight and drink of choice. Purely for entertainment — please continue to enjoy your coffees responsibly and one at a time.

faq.q: How is the friendship score calculated?
faq.a: It uses a fun algorithm that combines the letters of both names to generate a deterministic score. The same two names always produce the same result. This is purely for entertainment!

faq.q: Is this calculator accurate?
faq.a: Absolutely not. Real friendship is built on trust, shared experiences, and being there for each other — not a name algorithm. But it is fun to share with your mates.

howItWorks: Just like the love calculator, but for your mates. Type in two names and find out your official Friendship Compatibility Score™. Use it to confirm what you already knew: your best mate is top-tier. Or use it to stir the pot in the group chat. We won't judge.

title: Angel Number Calculator
description: Enter your birth date to discover your angel number and its meaning. Numerology made simple.
metaDescription: Angel number calculator — discover your angel number from your birth date. Find out what 111, 222, 333, 444, and other angel numbers mean for you.

faq.q: What is an angel number?
faq.a: Angel numbers are repeating number sequences (like 111, 222, 333) that some people believe carry spiritual significance. In numerology, your personal angel number is derived from your birth date by reducing it to a single digit or master number (11, 22, 33).

faq.q: How is the angel number calculated?
faq.a: All digits of your birth date are added together repeatedly until a single digit remains (or a master number 11, 22, or 33). For example: 15/03/1990 → 1+5+0+3+1+9+9+0 = 28 → 2+8 = 10 → 1+0 = 1. Your angel number would be 1.

faq.q: Is numerology real?
faq.a: Numerology is considered a pseudoscience with no scientific evidence supporting it. This calculator is for entertainment and curiosity purposes only. Have fun with it, but don't make life decisions based on the results!

howItWorks: Pop in your birthday and discover your angel number — the single digit (or rare master number) that numerology says reveals your life path. Are you a Leader, a Creative, or a Master Builder? Is it scientifically proven? Nope. Is it fun to read at 2am? Absolutely.

title: Egg Boiling Calculator
description: Get the perfect boiled egg every time. Soft, medium, or hard — adjusted for egg size and altitude.
metaDescription: Perfect egg boiling time calculator. Soft, medium, or hard boiled — adjusted for egg size, starting temperature, and altitude. Never over-boil again.
keywords: how long to boil an egg

faq.q: Why does altitude affect boiling time?
faq.a: Water boils at a lower temperature at higher altitudes (about 1°C less per 300m). This means eggs cook slower because the water is cooler, so you need to add extra time.

faq.q: Should I start with cold or boiling water?
faq.a: This calculator assumes you are lowering eggs into already-boiling water, which gives the most consistent results. If you start in cold water and bring to a boil, timing is less predictable.

faq.q: How do I get the perfect soft-boiled egg?
faq.a: Use a medium egg from the fridge, lower gently into rapidly boiling water, and cook for exactly 6 minutes 30 seconds. Transfer immediately to an ice bath to stop the cooking.

howItWorks: Runny, jammy, or hard? Getting the perfect boiled egg is weirdly stressful. This calculator takes the guesswork out — tell us how you like it, how big the egg is, and whether it's coming straight from the fridge. We'll give you the exact time. No more grey yolks. No more soupy whites. Just eggs, done right.

title: Lost Socks Calculator
description: How many socks have you lost to the washing machine in your lifetime? The numbers are devastating.
metaDescription: Lost socks calculator — find out how many socks you've probably lost to the washing machine over your lifetime. The answer will horrify you.

faq.q: Where do lost socks actually go?
faq.a: Research suggests socks get stuck behind the drum, sucked into the drain pump filter, trapped in fitted sheets or duvet covers, or kicked under/behind the machine. A Samsung study found the average person loses 1.3 socks per month.

faq.q: How much money do lost socks cost?
faq.a: At roughly -3 per pair, if you lose 15 socks per year that is about -45 annually in replacement socks. Over a lifetime, it adds up to hundreds of dollars.

faq.q: How can I stop losing socks?
faq.a: Use mesh laundry bags, pair socks before washing, use clips or sock locks, and always check inside fitted sheets and duvet covers before drying.

howItWorks: Where do they go? Seriously, where? One minute you've got a pair, next minute you've got a lonely orphan and a drawer full of mismatched disappointment. This calculator tallies up your estimated lifetime sock losses. The number might upset you. The cost definitely will.

title: Donut Calculator

metaDescription: Donut calculator — work out exactly how many donuts to buy for your office, party, or event. Accounts for donut lovers, light eaters, and dietary needs.

faq.q: How many donuts per person?
faq.a: The general rule is 1.5-2 donuts per adult for a casual setting. For a kids party, 1-1.5 each. For a breakfast meeting where donuts are the main event, 2-2.5 each.

faq.q: Should I get a variety?
faq.a: Absolutely. A good rule is 50% classic/glazed, 30% filled or specialty, and 20% chocolate. Always include at least one plain option for fussy eaters.

faq.q: How many dozen should I order?
faq.a: The calculator rounds up to the nearest half-dozen, because showing up with too many donuts is always better than too few. Nobody has ever complained about leftover donuts.

howItWorks: You've been asked to bring donuts. The pressure is real. Too few and you're the villain. Too many and... actually, has anyone ever complained about too many donuts? Tell us the occasion and how many people, and we'll sort out the rest. Including a suggested variety split, because nobody wants 36 cinnamon.

title: Beer Loss Calculator
description: How much beer is lost to foam, spillage, and mates who "forgot their wallet"? The ugly truth.
metaDescription: Beer loss calculator — calculate how much beer you lose to foam, spillage, warm dregs, and mates who never shout. Australian pub maths at its finest.

faq.q: How much beer is lost to foam?
faq.a: A properly poured beer loses about 5-10% of volume to the head. Over-poured tap beers can lose up to 15%. A bad pour at a busy pub on a Friday night? Could be 20%.

faq.q: What about warm dregs?
faq.a: Research suggests the average drinker leaves about 5% of each beer unfinished — the warm, flat bit at the bottom. Across a lifetime of drinking, this adds up to an alarming amount.

faq.q: How much do Aussies spend on beer?
faq.a: Australia is one of the most expensive countries in the world for beer. The average Australian adult drinks about 75 litres of beer per year, spending roughly ,000-3,000 annually.

howItWorks: Here's a painful truth: not every drop of beer you've paid for has made it to your mouth. Between the foam, the warm dregs you leave at the bottom, the odd spillage, and the rounds you've bought for mates who mysteriously vanish when it's their shout — the losses add up. This calculator shows you exactly how much. Sorry in advance.

faq.q: Is this based on real data?
faq.a: Yes. We scrape official NRL and AFL ladder standings daily. Your shit rating is calculated from actual ladder position, win/loss record, points differential, and current form streak. The roasts, however, are 100% editorial opinion.

faq.q: How often is the data updated?

faq.a: We're planning to add A-League, NBL, and eventually international leagues like the NBA, NFL, and Premier League. Stay tuned.

title: How Drunk Am I?
description: Had a few? Find out exactly where you sit on the scale from 'Sober King' to 'Talking to the Bins.'
metaDescription: Fun drunk calculator. Enter your drinks, time, and weight to find out how drunk you are on a cheeky scale. For entertainment only!

inputs.label: How many standard drinks?
inputs.help: 1 standard drink = 1 beer, 1 glass of wine, or 1 shot of spirits

howItWorks:
We use a modified version of the Widmark formula (the actual science behind blood alcohol estimation) and then translate the result into a far more useful scale than boring decimal numbers. Because nobody at the pub says "I'm at 0.08" — they say "I'm absolutely cooked."

Disclaimer: This is for entertainment only. Don't use this to decide whether you can drive. The answer is always: get an Uber.

faq.q: Is this medically accurate?
faq.a: No. This is a fun calculator for entertainment purposes only. Actual blood alcohol levels depend on dozens of factors we can't measure. Never use this to make decisions about driving or safety.

faq.q: What counts as a standard drink?
faq.a: In Australia, one standard drink contains 10g of alcohol. That's roughly one 285ml beer, one 100ml glass of wine, or one 30ml shot of spirits. Most pub pours are bigger than one standard drink.

faq.q: Why does weight matter?
faq.a: Alcohol distributes through body water. More body mass means more dilution, so larger people generally process the same amount of alcohol with a lower BAC. Biology is unfair like that.

howItWorks:
Rate your mate across six critical friendship dimensions. Our algorithm weighs each factor, applies the Mate Multiplier™, and delivers a brutally honest friendship grade.

The best part? You can share the results directly to your group chat. Because nothing strengthens a friendship like a public roasting.

faq.q: Should I send this to my mate?

faq.q: What if they rate me back?

title: Child Support Estimator
description: Estimate child support payments using the real Services Australia formula for 2025-26. Uses the cost-of-children table, self-support amount ($28,463), and official care percentage bands.
metaDescription: Estimate Australian child support payments for 2025-26 using the Services Australia formula. Accounts for income shares, care percentage bands, and the cost-of-children table.

inputs.label: Parent 1 — Nights per Fortnight with Children

faq.q: How is child support calculated in Australia?
faq.a: Services Australia uses an 8-step formula. It takes each parent's adjusted taxable income, subtracts the self-support amount ($28,463 for 2025-26), works out each parent's income share, applies the cost-of-children table for the combined income and age group, then adjusts for the care percentage each parent provides.

faq.q: What is the self-support amount?
faq.a: The self-support amount for 2025-26 is $28,463. This is deducted from each parent's adjusted taxable income before calculating child support, recognising that each parent needs a basic amount to live on.

faq.q: Does the number of nights really matter?
faq.a: Yes — nights per fortnight determine your care percentage band, which directly affects how much child support is payable. Even a one-night difference can move you into a different band with a significant dollar impact.

faq.q: Is this an official assessment?
faq.a: No. This is an estimate based on the published Services Australia formula and rates. Your actual assessment may differ based on factors like other dependants, multi-case caps, and any departure decisions. Use this as a guide and contact Services Australia for an official assessment.

howItWorks: This calculator applies the real Services Australia child support formula for 2025-26. It deducts the self-support amount ($28,463) from each parent's adjusted taxable income, calculates income shares, looks up the cost of children in the official table based on combined income and age group, then adjusts for your care percentage band. The result is an annual and fortnightly estimate of child support payable.

title: Care Percentage Calculator
description: Convert nights per fortnight into the official Services Australia care percentage and band. See how your care arrangement affects child support and Family Tax Benefit.
metaDescription: Convert nights per fortnight into the official Services Australia care percentage and band. See how care arrangements affect child support and Family Tax Benefit.

inputs.label: Does care change during school holidays?

faq.q: What are the care percentage bands?
faq.a: Services Australia uses five bands: Below regular care (0–13%), Regular care (14–34%), Shared care (35–47%), Shared care (48–52%), and Primary care (53–100%). Each band maps to a different cost percentage used in the child support formula.

faq.q: How do school holidays affect care percentage?
faq.a: If your care arrangement changes during holidays, Services Australia calculates a blended annual percentage. For example, 4 nights per fortnight in term time but 7 during holidays produces a higher annual average than 4 alone.

faq.q: What counts as a "night" of care?
faq.a: A night of care means the child sleeps at your home overnight. Daytime-only care does not count as a night, though significant daytime care can sometimes be recognised in special circumstances.

faq.q: Does care percentage affect Family Tax Benefit?
faq.a: Yes. Your FTB percentage is based on the same care determination. If you have at least 35% care, both parents can receive a share of FTB Part A proportional to their care percentage.

howItWorks: Enter your regular nights per fortnight and, if applicable, your holiday arrangement. The calculator converts nights into an annual care percentage using the Services Australia method, then maps it to the official care band. If holiday care differs, it blends term-time and holiday-time nights across the year to produce a weighted annual percentage.

title: Shared Expenses Splitter
description: Split shared child-related expenses fairly between separated parents based on income. Cover school fees, medical costs, activities, childcare, and more.
metaDescription: Split shared child expenses fairly between separated parents. Calculate proportional or 50-50 splits for school fees, medical, childcare, activities, and more.

faq.q: Should shared expenses be split 50-50 or by income?
faq.a: There's no single rule. Many families split proportionally to income so the higher earner covers a larger share, which is generally considered fairer. Others agree on 50-50 for simplicity. The best approach is whatever you can both agree on — this calculator lets you compare both.

faq.q: Are these expenses separate from child support?
faq.a: Yes. Child support covers the basic cost of raising children. Shared expenses like private school fees, orthodontics, and sporting activities are typically negotiated separately between parents, often documented in a parenting plan or consent order.

faq.q: What if one parent won't pay their share?
faq.a: If expenses are included in consent orders or a binding financial agreement, they're legally enforceable. If they're informal arrangements, you may need to apply to the court or negotiate through mediation. Services Australia can also include certain expenses in a child support agreement.

howItWorks: Enter both parents' incomes and all shared child-related expenses. The calculator totals the expenses and splits them according to your chosen method — proportional to income, straight 50-50, or a custom percentage. The result shows each parent's annual and monthly share for every expense category, plus the total.

title: Property Settlement Estimator
description: Estimate how assets and debts might be divided after separation. Calculate the total asset pool and see an indicative split range based on Australian family law.
metaDescription: Estimate how assets and debts are divided after separation under Australian family law. Calculate the total asset pool and see an indicative split range.

faq.q: How does property settlement work in Australia?
faq.a: Australian family law uses a four-step process: (1) identify and value the total asset pool, (2) assess each party's financial contributions, (3) consider future needs like caring for children and earning capacity, and (4) check whether the result is just and equitable overall.

faq.q: Is super included in the asset pool?
faq.a: Yes. Superannuation is treated as property under the Family Law Act and is included in the asset pool. It can be split via a superannuation splitting order, even before you reach retirement age.

faq.q: What split percentage is typical?
faq.a: There's no fixed rule. Outcomes range widely depending on contributions, relationship length, children, and future needs. Common ranges are 55/45 to 70/30, but every case is different. The primary carer of young children often receives an adjustment in their favour.

faq.q: Do I need a lawyer for property settlement?
faq.a: Not necessarily. If you and your ex agree, you can apply for consent orders ($195 filing fee) or make a binding financial agreement. However, getting independent legal advice is strongly recommended to ensure the agreement is fair and enforceable.

howItWorks: Enter the value of all major assets (property, super, savings, vehicles) and liabilities (mortgage, debts). The calculator totals the net asset pool, then applies an indicative split range based on relationship length, the primary carer role, income disparity, and the number of dependent children. The range reflects typical Australian family law outcomes — not a guaranteed result.

title: Separation Cost Planner
description: Calculate the realistic upfront costs of separating — rental bond, furniture, legal fees, mediation, moving costs, and setting up a new household.
metaDescription: Calculate the real upfront costs of separation in Australia. Covers rental bond, furniture, legal fees, mediation, moving costs, and setting up a new household.

faq.q: How much does it cost to separate in Australia?
faq.a: The upfront cost varies widely — from around $3,000 for a DIY separation with minimal setup, to $30,000+ if you need full furniture, professional movers, and solicitor representation. The biggest variables are legal fees, housing bond, and how much you need to furnish a new home.

faq.q: What are the court filing fees for divorce?
faq.a: The filing fee for a divorce application is $1,060 (2025-26). If you also need consent orders to formalise your property or parenting agreement, that's an additional $195. A reduced fee may apply if you have a Health Care Card or are in financial hardship.

faq.q: How much does mediation cost?
faq.a: Family Dispute Resolution (mediation) through a Family Relationship Centre is free or low-cost for the first session. Private mediators typically charge $200–$500 per hour. Lawyer-assisted mediation costs more but can resolve complex issues faster.

faq.q: Can I claim any of these costs on tax?
faq.a: Generally, no. Personal legal costs for family law matters are not tax-deductible. However, if part of the settlement involves income-producing assets (like an investment property), the legal costs related to that specific asset may be partially deductible. Get tax advice for your situation.

howItWorks: Select your situation across housing, furniture, moving, legal, and court filing categories. The calculator applies realistic Australian cost ranges to each selection and totals your estimated upfront separation costs. It also shows a breakdown by category so you can see where the biggest expenses are and plan accordingly.

title: Two-Household Budget Calculator
description: Compare your current combined household costs with the projected cost of running two separate homes after separation. See the real budget gap.
metaDescription: Compare the cost of one household versus two after separation. See the real budget gap and plan for running two homes on your combined income.

inputs.label: Current Utilities — power, gas, water, internet (monthly $)

faq.q: How much more does it cost to run two households?
faq.a: On average, running two separate households costs 30–50% more than a single combined household. The biggest driver is housing — rent or mortgage is typically the largest expense, and you're now paying it twice. Utilities, insurance, and groceries also increase, though not quite double.

faq.q: What expenses can be shared after separation?
faq.a: Some costs like children's school fees, medical expenses, and extracurricular activities can be split between parents. Streaming subscriptions, family phone plans, and bulk grocery shopping can also be coordinated. Use our Shared Expenses Splitter to work out fair shares.

faq.q: How do I close the budget gap?
faq.a: Common strategies include: downsizing the original home, choosing a more affordable rental, reducing discretionary spending, increasing income through extra work, applying for Family Tax Benefit and other government payments, and negotiating child support that reflects the real costs.

howItWorks: Enter your current combined household costs across six categories, then enter the projected costs for the new second household. The calculator compares your current single-household budget with the combined cost of running two homes, shows the dollar increase and percentage change, and compares the total against your combined after-tax income to reveal the budget gap.

title: Co-Parenting Schedule Calculator
description: Compare common parenting arrangements and see the care percentage, number of changeovers, and longest time away from each parent for each schedule.
metaDescription: Compare common Australian parenting schedules. See care percentages, changeovers, and time away for week-on/week-off, 5-2-2-5, 4-3, and other custody arrangements.

faq.q: What is the most common parenting arrangement in Australia?
faq.a: The most common arrangements are week-on/week-off (50/50) and every-other-weekend with midweek contact. The best arrangement depends on the children's ages, school logistics, parents' work schedules, and the distance between homes.

faq.q: What is a 5-2-2-5 schedule?
faq.a: In a 5-2-2-5 arrangement, the child spends 5 nights with Parent 1, then 2 with Parent 2, then 2 with Parent 1, then 5 with Parent 2. It's a 50/50 split with shorter maximum stretches away from each parent compared to week-on/week-off.

faq.q: How many changeovers is too many?
faq.a: There's no fixed rule, but frequent changeovers can be unsettling for younger children. Most family consultants suggest minimising mid-week transitions for children under 5. Older children and teenagers generally handle more changeovers without difficulty.

faq.q: Does the schedule affect child support?
faq.a: Yes. The number of nights per fortnight determines the care percentage band, which directly affects the child support calculation. A schedule change from 4 to 5 nights could move you into a different band and change the payment amount.

howItWorks: Select a parenting arrangement to see a detailed breakdown including the care percentage for each parent, the number of changeovers per fortnight, the longest consecutive stretch away from each parent, and the corresponding Services Australia care band. Compare different schedules to find the one that best suits your family's needs.

title: Child Support Change Calculator
description: See how changes in income, care percentage, or number of children would affect your child support assessment. Compare your current and proposed scenarios.
metaDescription: Compare how changes to income, care nights, or number of children affect your Australian child support assessment. Side-by-side current vs proposed scenarios.

faq.q: When can I apply for a change of assessment?
faq.a: You can apply for a change of assessment at any time if there's been a significant change in circumstances — such as a change in income, care arrangements, or the number of dependent children. You can also apply if you believe the formula assessment is unfair due to special circumstances.

faq.q: How long does a change of assessment take?
faq.a: An administrative change of assessment through Services Australia typically takes 6–12 weeks. If income details change (e.g. a new tax return is lodged), the reassessment can happen automatically within a few weeks.

faq.q: Does a change in care nights automatically change child support?
faq.a: Not automatically. You need to notify Services Australia of a change in care arrangements. If the care percentage changes by enough to move into a different band, your assessment will be recalculated from the date of notification.

faq.q: Can child support go up and down?
faq.a: Yes. Child support is recalculated whenever there's a change in either parent's income (usually when new tax returns are lodged), a change in care percentage, or a change in the number of children. It can increase or decrease depending on the circumstances.

howItWorks: Enter your current child support scenario (incomes, number of children, ages, and care nights) and then your proposed new scenario. The calculator runs the Services Australia formula on both and shows a side-by-side comparison of the annual and fortnightly child support amounts, highlighting the dollar and percentage change so you can see exactly how the proposed change would affect your assessment.
